raw_consumer_statement:
I have written to Amex at least 10 times via certified mail over the past 2 years, submitted photos, etc... I ordered specialty white oak wood tongue and groove floor planks from XXXX XXXX. Due to cc charge limits, I paid for aprox XXXX of the total charge ( $ XXXX ) on 1 amex cc account. When the order entered into the delivery phase of the transaction, I paid the remainder on another amex cc account ( {$6400.00} ). The shipment of the wood floor was horrendous. The floor planks were inadequately wrapped, the pallets used to transport the wood floor planks, were equally inadequate. 
I reported this as soon as the order arrived to my property. This damage was noted by the truck transport driver as 50 % damaged, and I DID NOT SIGN the delivery paperwork because it contained a preprinted statement that the merchandise arrived in acceptable condition. 
This occurred in XXXX of XXXX  The XXXX XXXX representative was also immediately notified about the 50 % damaged floor wood. Upon receiving the amex bill for {$6400.00}, which I calculated to account for the 50 % damaged floor wood, and the transport costs I paid to XXXX XXXX, I immediately disputed the charge on my amex credit card account. 
Again, I have sent at least 10 certified mail letters/photos, etc to Amex regarding this charge since XXXX, XXXX. Upon receipt of my certified mail, they zero out the charge, then I receive another statement re-issuing the charge. They totally ignore the facts... I did not secure the transport company, XXXX XXXX did. I did not use flimsy pallets that were unable to support the XXXX weight of the wood floor planks, XXXX XXXX made this flimsy decision. XXXXXXXX XXXX chose to use improper, flimsy shrink wrap to attempt to contain the stacked wood flooring. 
50 % of the wood flooring arrived with scarred finish, broked tongue and broken/cracked groove edges. All could have been prevented if... XXXX XXXX used ordinary care to wrap, support and ship in a considerate manner so the merchandise would arrive intact. They did not.
